seoTitle: How to Get Live Data From a Website in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn the basics of pulling live data from a website into Power BI, useful for tracking pricing, market trends, or public data feeds.
intro: Not every useful dataset comes as a clean file — sometimes the information you need only exists on a public webpage. This guide covers the basics of pulling live data from a website into Power BI using its web data connector. It's aimed at analysts who want to track things like pricing pages, public statistics, or other web-published data directly in their reports.

steps.heading: Set up your Power BI environment
steps.body: Open Power BI Desktop and prepare to use the Web connector, keeping in mind the target page's structure since that affects how easily the data can be pulled.

steps.heading: Access live web data sources
steps.body: Use Get Data, choose Web, and paste the URL of the page containing the data you want, letting Power BI attempt to detect tables automatically.

steps.heading: Work through practical examples
steps.body: Refine the imported data in Power Query, since web pages often bring in extra formatting or unrelated content alongside the table you actually want.

steps.heading: Automate data refreshes
steps.body: Set up a refresh schedule so the report periodically re-pulls the webpage, keeping the data reasonably current without manual re-importing.

faq.question: Can Power BI pull data from any website automatically?
faq.answer: It works best with pages that have clearly structured HTML tables; pages relying heavily on dynamic scripts or complex layouts can be much harder to extract data from.

faq.question: Is web scraping through Power BI legal for any website?
faq.answer: This depends entirely on the site's terms of service, so it's important to check that pulling data from a given source is permitted before setting up automated extraction.

faq.question: How often can the web data refresh automatically?
faq.answer: Refresh frequency depends on your Power BI license and workspace settings, with the Power BI service supporting scheduled refreshes at set intervals.

faq.question: What happens if the website's structure changes?
faq.answer: If the page layout changes significantly, the existing query may break or pull incorrect data, requiring you to update the Power Query steps to match the new structure.
